gene_region.label: IL33 transcribed interval
gene_region.definition: Canonical IL33 genomic interval on GRCh37 / hg19 from Ensembl GRCh37 lookup for ENSG00000137033 on the primary assembly, aligned to the hg19 coordinate system used by this app.

promoter_review_region.label: Operational promoter review window
promoter_review_region.definition: A practical 1 kb upstream window used by this app to flag promoter-adjacent coverage before the canonical gene interval begins.

promoter_hotspot_region.label: Promoter-associated CpG review window
promoter_hotspot_region.definition: Promoter-proximal CpG-focused span represented by the bundled EPIC subset and used here as the main methylation-review hotspot.

gene_context.gene_summary: IL33 encodes interleukin-33, an IL-1-family alarmin released from stressed epithelial, endothelial, and barrier tissues and sensed through the IL1RL1/ST2 receptor axis.
gene_context.clinical_context: The bundled IL33 knowledge base is focused on asthma, allergy, atopic dermatitis, airway inflammation, epithelial-barrier biology, and immune-regulatory research. Seeded markers and locus models are intended as research context and should not be promoted to diagnostic, prognostic, or treatment-selection claims without external clinical curation.
variant_effect_overview: IL33 variants are usually interpreted as regulatory or linked-locus modifiers of epithelial alarmin expression and type 2 inflammatory priming.
Asthma and allergy GWAS signals near IL33 should be read as low-penetrance susceptibility context, not as monogenic disease alleles.
condition_research_overview: Airway epithelial alarmin and type 2 inflammation research
gene_context.methylation_interpretation: IL33 methylation should be read as local regulatory context around an allergic-inflammation gene. Promoter-proximal or gene-body beta values may help frame accessibility or expression hypotheses, but they are not validated stand-alone biomarkers for asthma, eczema, allergy, immune activation, or treatment response.
methylation_effects: Promoter-proximal methylation may suggest a more restrained or permissive local regulatory state for IL33 expression potential.
Interpret methylation with tissue, cell composition, environmental exposure, disease state, medication history, and genotype context.
methylation_condition_research: Asthma, allergy, atopic dermatitis, eosinophilic inflammation, and airway epithelial studies.
Immune-cell, epithelial-barrier, cytokine-signaling, and inflammatory-disease epigenetic research.

evidence.label: Ensembl GRCh37 ENSG00000137033: IL33 coordinates and transcripts

variant_records.clinical_interpretation: rs1342326 is curated as an upstream/flanking A/C marker near IL33 for asthma and allergic-inflammation research. The A/C genomic alias is retained alongside the T/G reverse-complement notation used in parts of the literature, so strand notation should be checked before assigning direction.
variant_records.clinical_significance: Research-level allergic and immune-inflammatory association marker; not a deterministic clinical allele.
functional_effects: May tag regulatory, expression, coding, haplotype, or linked-locus variation around IL33.
Reported effects should be interpreted with ancestry, phenotype definition, tissue, and genotype dosage in mind.
associated_conditions: Airway epithelial alarmin and type 2 inflammation research
research_context: The marker sits about 25.7 kb upstream of the IL33 transcribed interval in the bundled GRCh37 coordinate context.
Use the A/C genomic REF/ALT alias and the T/G reverse-complement literature notation carefully when comparing sources.
Interpret rs1342326 as low-penetrance asthma and allergic-inflammation association context rather than as a diagnostic allele.
variant_records.usual_variant_note: IL33 marker used for allergic-inflammation research context.
variant_records.methylation_interpretation: IL33 methylation can provide local regulatory context, but it does not establish the variant, haplotype, expression state, or phenotype direction for rs1342326.

literature_findings.finding: The local curation treats rs1342326 as a research-grade IL33 signal for allergic, airway, epithelial-barrier, or immune-inflammatory biology.

variant_records.clinical_interpretation: rs2381416 is bundled as a IL33 research marker for allergic disease, asthma, eczema, airway inflammation, or immune-regulatory cohort literature. Treat it as low-penetrance, cohort-aware association context rather than as a diagnostic or treatment-selection allele.
variant_records.clinical_significance: Research-level allergic and immune-inflammatory association marker; not a deterministic clinical allele.
functional_effects: May tag regulatory, expression, coding, haplotype, or linked-locus variation around IL33.
Reported effects should be interpreted with ancestry, phenotype definition, tissue, and genotype dosage in mind.
associated_conditions: Airway epithelial alarmin and type 2 inflammation research
research_context: Use this marker as a research context flag and not as a stand-alone diagnosis.
Direction and effect size can vary across asthma, eczema, allergy, immune, epithelial, and ancestry-specific cohorts.
Confirm exact allele, build, zygosity, QC, and LD/haplotype context before drawing marker-level conclusions.

variant_records.methylation_interpretation: IL33 methylation can provide local regulatory context, but it does not establish the variant, haplotype, expression state, or phenotype direction for rs2381416.
